data.title: Scope-Aware Build Gate — auto-scope build/test/clippy to touched crates and stop false-aborting on pre-existing out-of-scope failures
data.executiveSummary: The super-dev build-gate (`src/build-runner.ts` + `src/stages/implementation.ts`) runs workspace-wide cargo build/test/clippy and then hard-terminates Stage 9 after 3 FAILs. On monorepos with PRE-EXISTING failures in crates the current branch never touched (e.g. stockfan-server spec 54: `reports_test.rs`, `stockfan-compute` clippy lints), this false-aborts Phase 1 while the phase's OWN code is green, so Phases 2–6 never run. Fix: (1) auto-derive the cargo package set from `git diff --merge-base main --name-only`, (2) scope ALL three gate commands (build + test + clippy) to that set, and (3) add an in-scope failure classification so the retry loop treats "all failures are pre-existing/out-of-scope" as phase-GREEN and only terminates-early on genuine in-scope failures. Backward compatible: empty scope or non-Cargo/non-git repos behave byte-identically to today.

acceptanceCriteria.statement: Add `detectTouchedCargoPackages(cwd, baseRef?): string[]` that spawns `git -C <cwd> diff --merge-base <baseRef> --name-only` (baseRef defaults to `"main"`, overridable via env `SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF`), maps every stdout line matching `crates/<pkg>/…` to `<pkg>`, dedupes preserving first-seen order (reuse the existing `dedupePreservingOrder` helper), and returns `[]` on ANY git error, empty diff, or a diff containing only non-crate paths. The helper MUST NEVER throw (try/catch the spawnSync). `runBuildGate` auto-derives the scoped set from touched crates when the caller passes no packages and `SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES` is unset.

Generalize `scopedCargoTestArgs` into a shared `scopedCargoArgs(subcommand: "build"|"test"|"clippy", packages: string[], extraArgs?: string[]): string[]` emitting `["cargo", subcommand, ...packages.flatMap(p => ["-p", p]), ...(extraArgs ?? [])]`. Keep `scopedCargoTestArgs(packages)` as a thin wrapper returning `scopedCargoArgs("test", packages, ["--quiet"])` (existing call sites in verify.ts/implementation.ts + any tests unchanged), and add `scopedCargoBuildArgs(packages)` (`["cargo","build",…,"--quiet"]` unscoped form) and `scopedCargoClippyArgs(packages)` (keeps `--all-targets --quiet`). Empty `packages` → byte-identical workspace-wide argv for all three (`["cargo","build","--quiet"]`, `["cargo","test","--quiet"]`, `["cargo","clippy","--all-targets","--quiet"]`).

In `runBuildGate`, when `cmds0.language === "rust"` AND the resolved package set is non-empty, override `build`, `test`, AND `typecheck` on the shallow-copy `cmds` object (today only `test` is overridden). When the set is empty, all three stay workspace-wide and the `cmds` object is byte-identical to current behavior. Package-set precedence (highest→lowest): explicit `opts.testPackages`/`opts.packages` (incl. explicit `[]` = force workspace-wide) → `process.env.SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES` → `detectTouchedCargoPackages(cwd)` → workspace-wide. Detecting touched crates must not run when a higher-precedence source is present.

Extend `BuildGateResult` with `outOfScopeErrors: string[]` and `inScopePass: boolean`. After collecting `errors`, classify each error block: extract every source path it references via the `crates/<pkg>/` segment inside `--> <path>` markers AND `-p <pkg>` markers in cargo test-failure lines; an error is OUT-OF-SCOPE iff EVERY referenced crate is NOT in the resolved scoped set, otherwise IN-SCOPE. Compute `inScopePass = !gate.pass && gate.errors.length > 0 && gate.outOfScopeErrors.length === gate.errors.length` (all failures pre-existing/out-of-scope). When `gate.pass` is true, `inScopePass` is true and classification is a no-op. The classifier must NEVER throw; on any ambiguity it treats the error as IN-SCOPE (conservative — does not grant false green).

In `implementation.ts` retry loop, a phase is GREEN when `gate.pass || gate.inScopePass`. When green via `inScopePass`, log: `Implementation ${phaseId} IN-SCOPE GREEN on attempt ${attempt} — ${gate.outOfScopeErrors.length} pre-existing out-of-scope failure(s) ignored (crates: <comma-list>)`. The `if (!green) { … "failed after N attempts — terminating early"; allGreen=false; break; }` path fires ONLY when NEITHER pass nor inScopePass holds after MAX_ATTEMPTS (genuine in-scope failures). `runBuildGate(setup.worktreePath, { signal: ctx.signal })` call signature unchanged — scoping is derived internally.

Backward-compatibility / non-regression: non-Cargo repos, non-git dirs, no touched crates, and unset env vars produce IDENTICAL gate argvs and an IDENTICAL `runBuildGate` result to today (modulo the two additive fields), and the new in-scope classification never blocks when there are zero failures or no scoping is active (workspace-wide ⇒ every failure counts as in-scope ⇒ `inScopePass` stays false, preserving current abort semantics). `detectProjectCommands` purity is preserved (scoping applied only on the shallow copy).

New co-located unit/integration tests in `src/build-runner.test.ts` (and/or an `implementation` test) covering: (a) `detectTouchedCargoPackages` path→pkg mapping, dedupe, order preservation, git-failure→`[]`, non-crate-only-diff→`[]`, base-ref override via env; (b) `scopedCargoBuildArgs`/`scopedCargoTestArgs`/`scopedCargoClippyArgs` argv construction including empty-set→byte-identical workspace-wide; (c) in-scope classification with scoped set `{data}` where a failure in `crates/data/…` is IN-SCOPE and a failure in `crates/compute/…` is OUT-OF-SCOPE, plus `inScopePass` true only when ALL failures are out-of-scope; (d) a `runBuildGate` integration that stubs `spawnSync`/git-diff so a touched-`data` crate drives all three commands to carry `-p data`. `npm run typecheck` (tsc --noEmit, strict) and `npm test` (vitest run) — existing AND new — all pass. No new runtime dependencies; no new spawned processes beyond existing gate commands plus one `git diff --name-only`.

nonFunctional: Performance / cost: Auto-scoping adds at most ONE additional spawnSync per gate run (`git -C <cwd> diff --merge-base <baseRef> --name-only`) and is skipped entirely when a higher-precedence package source is present; on large monorepos it REDUCES wall-time by scoping the three cargo commands. The git-diff helper must use the existing spawnSync pattern with the resolved timeout envelope, never an unbounded shell.

Robustness / safety: `detectTouchedCargoPackages` and the in-scope classifier MUST NEVER throw — any git error, malformed output, or regex ambiguity degrades to workspace-wide behavior / conservative in-scope classification. This preserves the 'never false-abort on green, never false-pass on broken' contract.

Backward-compatibility / determinism: empty resolved scope ⇒ byte-identical argvs and result to today; the two new `BuildGateResult` fields are additive and no-op when there are no failures or no active scoping. `detectProjectCommands` stays pure (overrides only on the shallow copy).

Constraint isolation: pure TS change to `src/build-runner.ts`, `src/stages/implementation.ts`, and tests only — nodes.ts / workflow.ts / pipeline.ts / render templates / control-flow engine untouched. Target repo never mutated by the gate.
No new runtime dependencies; no new spawned processes beyond existing gate commands + one `git diff --name-only`.
openQuestions: When the resolved scoped set is EMPTY (workspace-wide), should `inScopePass` be forced false so the new field can NEVER grant a green the old code wouldn't? RECOMMENDED: yes — treat workspace-wide as 'every failure is in-scope', so `inScopePass` is only ever true when active cargo scoping produced an all-out-of-scope failure set. This keeps non-Cargo and non-scoped runs byte-identical.

`detectTouchedCargoPackages` reads `git diff --merge-base main` which reflects COMMITTED changes in the worktree. If the implementer created a brand-new crate not yet committed, it won't be scoped. RECOMMENDED: accept this and rely on the per-phase commit step having run before the gate (current implementation.ts commits AFTER green, so the FIRST attempt may run before the new crate is committed) — note as a known edge case; fallback in-scope classification still protects against false-abort.

Base-ref default is literally `"main"`. Some repos default to `master`/`develop`. RECOMMENDED: ship `"main"` as the literal default per spec (env `SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF` covers the rest) and note default-branch auto-detection as future work; if `main` doesn't exist, `git diff --merge-base` fails and the helper returns `[]` (safe workspace-wide fallback).

Should the in-scope classification also be surfaced into the log/stderr tail consumed by the implementer's next attempt (so it does NOT retry against pre-existing failures)? RECOMMENDED: yes — when `inScopePass` is the reason for green, the retry loop does not run another attempt, so no fix-loop waste; when genuine in-scope failures remain, feed only the IN-SCOPE subset into `attemptErrors` so the implementer fixes real failures, not pre-existing noise.

data.title: Scope-Aware Build Gate — auto-scope cargo gate to touched crates and stop false-aborting on pre-existing out-of-scope failures

features.name: Auto-detection of cargo crates touched by the branch

scenarios.title: Touched-crate detection maps changed crate paths to package names preserving order
scenarios.given: a git worktree whose current branch changed files under crates/data/ and crates/api/ relative to base ref main
scenarios.when: the build gate derives the touched cargo package set
scenarios.then: the resolved set is [data, api] in first-seen order with duplicates removed

scenarios.title: Base ref can be overridden via the environment
scenarios.given: the environment variable SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF is set to develop
scenarios.when: touched-crate detection runs
scenarios.then: the branch is compared against develop instead of the default main

scenarios.title: Git failures, empty diffs, and non-crate-only diffs degrade safely to workspace-wide
scenarios.given: a non-git directory, a missing base ref, an empty diff, or a diff containing only non-crate paths
scenarios.when: touched-crate detection runs
scenarios.then: the resolved set is empty
andClauses: the helper never throws an exception
the gate falls back to workspace-wide behavior

features.name: Scoped cargo argv construction for build, test, and clippy

scenarios.title: Scoped helpers emit per-package -p flags with preserved extra flags
scenarios.given: a non-empty resolved package set {data, api}
scenarios.when: the scoped build, test, and clippy argvs are constructed
scenarios.then: each argv starts with its cargo subcommand followed by -p data -p api and the expected extra flags
andClauses: the test argv keeps the --quiet flag
the clippy argv keeps --all-targets and --quiet
the build argv carries --quiet
the existing scopedCargoTestArgs callers and tests remain unchanged

scenarios.title: Empty package set produces byte-identical workspace-wide argvs
scenarios.given: an empty resolved package set
scenarios.when: the three scoped helpers construct their argvs
scenarios.then: the build, test, and clippy argvs are byte-identical to today's workspace-wide forms
andClauses: cargo build --quiet
cargo test --quiet
cargo clippy --all-targets --quiet

scenarios.title: Build, test, and clippy are all scoped to touched crates when nothing is overridden
scenarios.given: a cargo workspace whose branch touched crates/data and no explicit or environment package override is provided
scenarios.when: the build gate resolves the command set
scenarios.then: the build, test, and typecheck (clippy) argvs all carry the -p data scoping on the shallow command copy

scenarios.title: Higher-precedence package sources skip auto-detection and win
scenarios.given: explicit test packages, an explicit empty array, or the SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES environment variable is present
scenarios.when: the build gate resolves the scoped package set
scenarios.then: the higher-precedence source is used and touched-crate detection is not run at all
andClauses: an explicit empty array forces workspace-wide
auto-detection is never invoked when an override exists

scenarios.title: Empty resolved scope leaves all three commands workspace-wide and unchanged
scenarios.given: an empty resolved package set
scenarios.when: the build gate builds its command object
scenarios.then: the build, test, and typecheck argvs are byte-identical to the detected project commands

features.name: In-scope failure classification on gate result

scenarios.title: All-out-of-scope failures yield an in-scope pass
scenarios.given: a failed gate whose collected errors all reference crates outside the resolved scoped set {data}, such as crates/compute/
scenarios.when: the gate classifies each error block
scenarios.then: every error is recorded as out-of-scope
andClauses: outOfScopeErrors holds all of them
inScopePass is true because every failure is pre-existing/out-of-scope

scenarios.title: Any in-scope failure prevents an in-scope pass
scenarios.given: a failed gate whose errors include at least one referencing a scoped crate, e.g. crates/data/
scenarios.when: the gate classifies the errors
scenarios.then: that error is classified as in-scope
andClauses: inScopePass is false (no false green)
errors in crates/compute/ alongside the in-scope one are still recorded in outOfScopeErrors

scenarios.title: Ambiguous or malformed errors are treated conservatively as in-scope
scenarios.given: an error block that cannot be reliably mapped to any crate, or malformed classifier output
scenarios.when: the gate classifies it
scenarios.then: the error is treated as in-scope rather than granting a false green
andClauses: the classifier never throws
a passing gate sets inScopePass true and skips classification as a no-op

features.name: Implementation retry loop treats in-scope pass as phase green

scenarios.title: Out-of-scope-only failures are treated as phase green without early termination
scenarios.given: a phase whose gate reports inScopePass true with several pre-existing out-of-scope failures
scenarios.when: the implementation retry loop decides the phase verdict
scenarios.then: the phase is considered green
andClauses: the gate call signature passed to the worktree is unchanged (scoping derived internally)
the log states IN-SCOPE GREEN with the count of ignored out-of-scope failures and their crates
the loop does not waste further attempts

scenarios.title: Genuine in-scope failures still terminate the stage early
scenarios.given: a phase whose gate fails with at least one in-scope error on every attempt up to the maximum
scenarios.when: the maximum number of attempts is reached without pass or inScopePass
scenarios.then: the stage terminates early reporting failure after the attempts
andClauses: allGreen is set false and remaining phases do not run
early termination fires only on genuine in-scope failures

scenarios.title: A cleanly passing gate is green via pass regardless of classification
scenarios.given: a phase whose gate passes outright
scenarios.when: the retry loop checks the verdict
scenarios.then: the phase is green via pass and proceeds normally

scenarios.title: Non-cargo, non-git, and unset-config repos behave identically to today
scenarios.given: a non-cargo repository, a non-git directory, a repo with no touched crates, or all environment overrides unset
scenarios.when: the build gate runs
scenarios.then: the gate argvs and result are identical to today's behavior
andClauses: the only differences are the two additive result fields
project-command detection purity is preserved (overrides apply only on the shallow copy)
the in-scope classification never blocks when there are no failures or no scoping is active

scenarios.title: Workspace-wide scope never grants an in-scope pass the old code would not
scenarios.given: a workspace-wide (empty-scoped) gate run
scenarios.when: failures occur
scenarios.then: every failure counts as in-scope so inScopePass stays false
andClauses: current abort semantics are preserved
non-scoped runs cannot gain a green they would not have had

scenarios.title: New co-located tests cover detection, argv construction, classification, and integration
scenarios.given: the new build-runner and implementation tests covering touched-crate detection, scoped argv construction, in-scope classification, and a stubbed runBuildGate integration
scenarios.when: the test suite runs
scenarios.then: both existing and new tests pass

scenarios.title: Strict typecheck and full test run pass with no new dependencies
scenarios.given: the strict TypeScript project with no new runtime dependencies or spawned processes beyond the existing gate commands plus one git diff --name-only
scenarios.when: typecheck and tests run
scenarios.then: npm run typecheck (tsc --noEmit, strict) and npm test (vitest run) both succeed
andClauses: no new runtime dependencies are introduced
no new processes beyond existing gate commands plus one git diff --name-only

scenarios.title: Constraint isolation is maintained
scenarios.given: a change limited to src/build-runner.ts, src/stages/implementation.ts, and tests
scenarios.when: the change set is reviewed
scenarios.then: nodes.ts, workflow.ts, pipeline.ts, render templates, and the control-flow engine remain untouched
andClauses: the target repository is never mutated by the gate

scenarios.title: Auto-scoping adds at most one git diff per gate and reduces wall-time on monorepos
scenarios.given: a large monorepo with a higher-precedence package source absent
scenarios.when: the gate runs with auto-detected touched crates
scenarios.then: at most one additional git diff --name-only spawn occurs
andClauses: the git-diff helper uses the existing bounded spawnSync pattern, never an unbounded shell
the three scoped cargo commands reduce overall wall-time versus workspace-wide

scenarios.title: Robustness helpers never throw and always degrade safely
scenarios.given: any git error, malformed classifier output, or regex ambiguity during detection or classification
scenarios.when: the gate continues
scenarios.then: it degrades to workspace-wide behavior or a conservative in-scope classification
andClauses: the helper and classifier never throw
the contract of never false-aborting on green and never false-passing on broken is preserved

features.name: Known edge cases and explicitly out-of-scope work

scenarios.title: Uncommitted brand-new crate relies on fallback classification rather than detection
scenarios.given: an implementer created a new crate that has not yet been committed before the first gate attempt
scenarios.when: touched-crate detection runs against committed changes
scenarios.then: the new crate is not in the resolved set
andClauses: the in-scope fallback classification still protects against false-abort
this remains a documented known edge case

scenarios.title: Full main-baseline diff gating is explicitly deferred to future work
scenarios.given: the current in-scope classification approach
scenarios.when: the implementation summary is written
scenarios.then: running the gate on main and subtracting baselines is noted as a future enhancement rather than implemented this pass
andClauses: default-branch auto-detection beyond the literal main default is also noted as future work

scenarios.title: Genuine in-scope failures are the only errors fed back to the implementer
scenarios.given: a gate run with mixed in-scope and out-of-scope failures during a genuine in-scope retry
scenarios.when: the implementer's next attempt receives its error context
scenarios.then: only the in-scope subset is surfaced for fixing
andClauses: pre-existing out-of-scope noise is excluded from the feedback
when inScopePass is the reason for green, no further attempt is made, avoiding fix-loop waste

scenarios.title: In-scope green is logged with the ignored crate list
scenarios.given: a phase that is green via inScopePass on attempt N
scenarios.when: the retry loop records the verdict
scenarios.then: the log message identifies the phase and attempt, states IN-SCOPE GREEN, and lists the count and comma-separated crate names of ignored pre-existing out-of-scope failures
andClauses: the message format matches: Implementation <phaseId> IN-SCOPE GREEN on attempt <attempt> — <count> pre-existing out-of-scope failure(s) ignored (crates: <list>)

scenarios.title: Baseline subtraction on main is not implemented this pass
scenarios.given: the recommendation to ship the cheaper in-scope classification now
scenarios.when: work is completed
scenarios.then: full baseline-diff gating is documented as future work in the implementation summary
andClauses: no baseline-diff code paths are introduced
the in-scope classification covers the common case more cheaply

scenarios.title: Existing scopedCargoTestArgs callers behave identically via the shared helper
scenarios.given: existing call sites and tests for scopedCargoTestArgs in verify.ts and implementation.ts
scenarios.when: scopedCargoTestArgs is reimplemented as a thin wrapper over the shared scopedCargoArgs
scenarios.then: those callers and tests continue to pass without modification
andClauses: scopedCargoTestArgs(packages) returns scopedCargoArgs("test", packages, ["--quiet"])
new scopedCargoBuildArgs and scopedCargoClippyArgs are added without changing the existing function's contract

scenarios.title: Both source-path and -p package markers are recognized when classifying
scenarios.given: cargo error blocks that reference crates via crates/<pkg>/ path segments inside --> <path> markers and via -p <pkg> markers in test-failure lines
scenarios.when: the classifier extracts referenced crates
scenarios.then: all referenced crates from both marker styles are considered when deciding in- vs out-of-scope
andClauses: an error is out-of-scope only when every referenced crate is outside the scoped set
an error touching at least one scoped crate is in-scope

scenarios.title: Strict mode type-clean across the changed files
scenarios.given: the strict tsconfig and the new BuildGateResult fields plus the new helper signatures
scenarios.when: tsc --noEmit runs under strict mode
scenarios.then: no type errors are reported in build-runner.ts, implementation.ts, or their tests

traceability.description: detectTouchedCargoPackages(cwd, baseRef?) spawns git diff --merge-base <baseRef> --name-only, maps crates/<pkg>/ lines to packages, dedupes preserving order, returns [] on any git error/empty/non-crate diff, never throws; runBuildGate auto-derives the scoped set when no override is given.

Generalize scopedCargoTestArgs into scopedCargoArgs(subcommand, packages, extraArgs?); keep scopedCargoTestArgs as a thin wrapper and add scopedCargoBuildArgs and scopedCargoClippyArgs; empty set yields byte-identical workspace-wide argvs.

In runBuildGate, override build/test/typecheck on the shallow cmds copy when rust and non-empty scope; empty scope stays byte-identical; precedence explicit -> SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES -> touched crates -> workspace-wide; detection skipped when higher source present.

Extend BuildGateResult with outOfScopeErrors and inScopePass; classify errors via crates/<pkg>/ and -p <pkg> markers; inScopePass true only when all failures are out-of-scope; classifier never throws and treats ambiguity as in-scope.

implementation.ts retry loop: phase is GREEN when gate.pass || gate.inScopePass; IN-SCOPE GREEN logged; early-terminate only on genuine in-scope failures after MAX_ATTEMPTS; runBuildGate call signature unchanged; existing scopedCargoTestArgs callers/tests unchanged.

Backward compatibility: non-Cargo/non-git/no-touched/unset-env runs identical to today modulo additive fields; workspace-wide never grants inScopePass; detectProjectCommands purity preserved; full baseline-diff gating deferred.

New co-located tests covering detection, argv construction, classification, and a stubbed runBuildGate integration; npm run typecheck (strict) and npm test (vitest) pass; no new deps/processes; constraint isolation maintained.

data.title: Scope-aware build-gate: resolving crate-scoping + in-scope-classification open issues
data.summary: Follow-up research resolving the 5 open issues (ISS-001..ISS-005) blocking the scope-aware build-gate task. All five resolve to ACCEPTED design decisions with safe degradation — none block implementation, so openIssues is empty. ISS-001 (crate-name vs directory): Cargo's `-p spec`/`--package spec` selects by package NAME, not path (Cargo book, SRC-001), so a member at `crates/foo/` named `bar` makes `cargo -p foo` unresolved; this is safe for stockfan-server where dir==name by convention and is accepted as a known limitation this pass — the robust future fix is `cargo metadata --no-deps --format-version 1` (authoritative member name + manifest_path map), NOT raw `[workspace].members` parsing, because `[workspace].members` lists path globs, not package names. ISS-002 (layout): path-prefix scoping `crates/<pkg>/` means libs/xtask/bin/root-crate diffs yield `[]` → workspace-wide fallback (AC-01); no false scope, just lost scoping benefit — documented limitation. ISS-003 (git context): `git diff --merge-base <commit>` is exactly `git diff $(git merge-base <commit> HEAD)` (SRC-002); an unresolved ref (shallow/disconnected clone, detached HEAD, unborn branch, non-main default) exits non-zero → AC-01's try/catch degrades to `[]` → workspace-wide, and SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF covers non-main defaults; AC-07a's git-error→[] test pins this. ISS-004 (stderr tail): STDERR_TAIL_LINES=12 (build-runner.ts:147); a failure whose `--> <path>`/`-p <pkg>` marker falls outside the captured tail is unclassifiable → treated IN-SCOPE (conservative), so it never grants a false green but may cause an unnecessary retry/terminate — accepted; a cheap near-term mitigation is bumping the tail (12→~40), full fix is the deferred baseline-diff (#3). ISS-005 (clippy --all-targets): Cargo `-p` (package/workspace-member filter) and `--all-targets` (target-KIND filter = --lib --bins --tests --benches --examples, SRC-001) are orthogonal and order-insensitive, so `cargo clippy -p data --all-targets --quiet` lints every target kind of the `data` package while preserving the required `--all-targets --quiet` flags (AC-02) — confirmed correct. Net: the touched-set + conservative in-scope classifier design is sound and ready to implement; the in-scope verdict (gate.pass || gate.inScopePass) correctly stops the false-abort on pre-existing out-of-scope failures without ever risking a false green.

options.name: ISS-001 crate-name mapping: dir-name heuristic now, cargo metadata later (CHOSEN)
options.tradeoffs: This pass maps `crates/<pkg>/` -> `-p <pkg>` assuming dir==package-name, which holds for stockfan-server by convention. Verified that `-p` is name-based (SRC-001), so divergence makes `cargo -p <dir>` unresolved rather than silently wrong — i.e. a hard gate failure, not a false pass, so the failure mode is safe-strict. Pros: zero new deps, one git diff, byte-identical backward compat. Con: loses scoping (falls to workspace-wide) or hard-fails the gate if a repo's dir!=name, which re-triggers the very false-abort we're fixing. ACCEPTED for this pass; robust future fix is `spawnSync('cargo', ['metadata','--no-deps','--format-version','1'])` and parse members' `name`+`manifest_path` for an authoritative path->name map — avoid hand-parsing `[workspace].members` since it lists path globs, not names. Note in 10-implementation-summary.md as future hardening.

options.name: ISS-004 classifier posture: conservative default-IN-SCOPE with modest tail bump (CHOSEN)
options.tradeoffs: For a HARD test oracle the asymmetry favors false-fail over false-pass: a false pass ships a regression, a false fail only costs a retry. Defaulting ambiguous/unparseable failures (incl. those truncated outside STDERR_TAIL_LINES=12) to IN-SCOPE, and granting inScopePass only when ALL failures map to untouched crates, guarantees no false green. Pros: cheap, never dangerous. Con: may cause an unnecessary retry/terminate when a real (in-scope) failure's path marker is clipped by the 12-line tail. Mitigation: bump STDERR_TAIL_LINES to ~40 (cheap, still bounded) to widen classification coverage; full layout/format-independence is the deferred baseline-diff (#3). ACCEPTED.

options.name: ISS-003 base ref: git merge-base (default main, env-overridable) + total try/catch (CHOSEN)
options.tradeoffs: `git diff --merge-base <commit>` diffs against the common ancestor of <commit> and HEAD (SRC-002), so a branch that rebased/merged main compares at the right point — more correct than a bare two-dot `main..HEAD`, which drifts/empties after a rebase. An unresolved ref (shallow/disconnected/detached/unborn/non-main) exits non-zero; AC-01's try/catch->[] degrades to workspace-wide and SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF covers non-main defaults. Pros: rebase-safe, total (never throws), env-escapable. Con: needs `main` (or configured ref) present; covered by the git-error->[] test (AC-07a). ACCEPTED.

options.name: ISS-005 clippy scoping: preserve --all-targets --quiet, -p is orthogonal (CONFIRMED)
options.tradeoffs: Cargo docs (SRC-001) show `-p spec` filters which packages and `--all-targets` filters which target KINDS (lib+bins+tests+benches+examples); they are independent axes and order-insensitive. So `cargo clippy -p data --all-targets --quiet` lints every target kind of `data` exactly as the workspace-wide form lints every kind — no loss of coverage, no behavior change beyond scoping the package set. scopedCargoClippyArgs(packages) should emit `[

options.name: ISS-002 layout coverage: crates/ prefix with graceful [] fallback (CONFIRMED)
options.tradeoffs: Scoping triggers only for `crates/<pkg>/...` diffs; libs/xtask/bin/root-crate edits yield no match -> [] -> workspace-wide (AC-01). This is safe-by-design: no false scope is ever applied, so behavior is byte-identical to today for non-crates diffs, and the in-scope classifier then counts every failure as IN-SCOPE (no false green). Pro: simplest correct scoping that needs no manifest parsing. Con: loses scoping benefit on non-`crates/` layouts. ACCEPTED as documented limitation; a future layout-aware detector could consult `[workspace].members` globs, but that overlaps with the ISS-001 cargo-metadata hardening.

data.title: RCA: scope-blind build-gate false-fails then false-aborts on pre-existing out-of-scope failures
data.summary: On the 2026-07-19 stockfan-server spec-54 run, the timeout fix worked and Phase 1's OWN code was demonstrably green (agents verified 273 stockfan-data + 10 stockfan-api tests via `cargo test -p …`), yet Stage 9 implementation aborted after Phase 1 ("phase-01 failed after 3 attempts — terminating early"), so Phases 2–6 never ran. The build-gate reported FAIL ×3 on three pre-existing, out-of-scope failures: `reports_test.rs` (a different Reports-API feature), `stockfan-compute` clippy lints 18–30 (a different crate's tech debt), and `job_queries_test.rs`. Root cause is two compounding scope-blind defects. (A) `runBuildGate` (src/build-runner.ts:282) scopes ONLY `cmds0.test` at line 301 — `build` and `typecheck` (clippy) stay workspace-wide — and even that `test` scoping is gated on an explicit/env package list that was never set in this run, so all three commands ran workspace-wide `cargo build && cargo test && cargo clippy --all-targets`. (B) The implementation retry loop (src/stages/implementation.ts:43–78) has a binary 3-strikes-terminate: it retries up to MAX_ATTEMPTS=3 feeding `gate.errors` back to the implementer each round, but those errors are pre-existing/out-of-scope — the implementer cannot fix them — so all three attempts repeat-FAIL, then `if (!green) { … break; }` at :74–78 aborts ALL remaining phases. The gate cannot tell "my failures" from "the repo's pre-existing failures," so it false-aborts on green code. The fix set addresses both: (1) `detectTouchedCargoPackages(cwd, baseRef)` auto-derives the scoped crate set from `git diff --merge-base main --name-only`; (2) generalize `scopedCargoTestArgs` → `scopedCargoArgs("build"|"test"|"clippy", …)` so build+test+clippy ALL carry `-p` when the resolved set is non-empty (empty → byte-identical workspace-wide); (3) add `outOfScopeErrors[]` + `inScopePass:boolean` to `BuildGateResult` (classify by `crates/<pkg>/` from `--> <path>` markers and `-p <pkg>` markers; conservative → in-scope on ambiguity); (4) implementation.ts treats `gate.pass || gate.inScopePass` as phase-GREEN and only terminates-early on genuine in-scope failures. Backward-compatible: non-cargo, non-git, no-touched-crates, and unset-env paths produce identical results to today (the two additive fields never block when there are zero failures).
hypotheses: H1 (70%): The gate ran all three commands workspace-wide. No scoping source was active (no `SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES` env, implementation.ts:64 passes no `opts.testPackages`), AND even when scoping IS active commit 97fc4df6 only overrides `cmds.test` (build-runner.ts:301) leaving `build` and `clippy` workspace-wide. Prediction: out-of-scope crates' pre-existing failures (reports_test.rs, stockfan-compute clippy lints, job_queries_test.rs) flow into `gate.errors`; setting SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES=stockfan-data would still leave cargo build and cargo clippy workspace-wide and FAIL. Confirmed by code read: only `cmds.test` is overridden.

H2 (20%): Even with a touched-crate-scoped gate, the retry loop (implementation.ts:74–78) treats ANY non-pass as hard failure → `break` after 3 attempts → false-abort, because there is no in-scope/out-of-scope distinction. The implementer cannot fix pre-existing out-of-scope failures, so all 3 attempts repeat-FAIL identically. Prediction: a scoped gate that still leaks one out-of-scope failure (e.g. via a dependency compile/clippy ripple) still aborts the run. This is the second-order defect the in-scope classifier must defuse.

H3 (8%): A genuine in-scope regression the implementer could not fix in 3 attempts. Prediction: re-running Phase 1's own `cargo test -p stockfan-data -p stockfan-api` in isolation FAILS. FALSIFIED by the run log — agents verified 273+10 tests green via package-scoped cargo, so the branch's own code passed.

H4 (2%): Environmental flake (ENOENT, race, ETIMEDOUT). Prediction: failures are non-deterministic / tied to missing binaries. Largely falsified: the timeout fix produced no ETIMEDOUT, and the three failures are deterministic, named test files / clippy lints, not transient.
data.rootCause:
Two compounding scope-blindness defects, neither of which can distinguish the branch's own failures from the repo's pre-existing failures:

(1) OVER-SCOPING OF COMMANDS — src/build-runner.ts `runBuildGate` (line 282) builds the shallow-copy `cmds` by overriding ONLY `cmds0.test` at line 301 (`{ ...cmds0, test: scopedCargoTestArgs(testPackages) }`). `build` and `typecheck` (clippy) are never scoped. Worse, `testPackages` resolves ONLY from `opts.testPackages` or `process.env.SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES` (lines 291–294); there is no auto-derivation from what the branch actually touched. The implementation caller (src/stages/implementation.ts:64) invokes `runBuildGate(setup.worktreePath, { signal: ctx.signal })` with no packages and the env var was unset, so all three commands ran the blunt workspace-wide `cargo build && cargo test && cargo clippy --all-targets`, dragging in the pre-existing `reports_test.rs`, `stockfan-compute` clippy lints, and `job_queries_test.rs` failures that live in crates the branch never modified. Confirmed by grep: no `scopedCargoBuildArgs`/`scopedCargoClippyArgs`/`detectTouchedCargoPackages` exist; only `scopedCargoTestArgs` is defined (line 142) and called once (line 301).

(2) BINARY 3-STRIKES-TERMINATE — src/stages/implementation.ts retry loop (lines 43–78) sets `green=false`, loops `attempt 1..MAX_ATTEMPTS(=3)`, feeds the previous attempt's `gate.errors` back into the implementer prompt each round (line 54), and on each non-pass logs "attempt N/3 FAIL". Because the failures are pre-existing/out-of-scope, the implementer cannot remove them, so all three attempts FAIL identically. Then line 74 `if (!green) { … "failed after N attempts — terminating early"; allGreen=false; break; }` fires: the `break` exits the phase loop and aborts ALL remaining phases (2–6). There is no in-scope classifier and no `inScopePass` field on `BuildGateResult`, so the loop has no way to treat an all-out-of-scope failure as phase-green.

Composition: defect (1) injects out-of-scope failures into `gate.errors`; defect (2) escalates those into a hard abort. The fix mirrors this: (1) auto-scope build+test+clippy to touched crates via `detectTouchedCargoPackages` + a generalized `scopedCargoArgs`, and (2) add `outOfScopeErrors`/`inScopePass` classification so `gate.pass || gate.inScopePass` is treated as GREEN and only genuine in-scope failures terminate early.
reproductionSteps: Minimal code-level repro (confirms defect A + missing helpers): grep -n 'scopedCargoBuildArgs\|scopedCargoClippyArgs\|detectTouchedCargoPackages\|inScopePass\|outOfScopeErrors' src/build-runner.ts src/stages/implementation.ts → all absent; confirm src/build-runner.ts:301 only overrides `cmds.test` and src/stages/implementation.ts:64 passes no packages. This alone proves build+clippy run workspace-wide and the retry loop is scope-blind.

Workspace repro (mirrors the stockfan run): in a Rust workspace monorepo (root Cargo.toml, crates under crates/), seed a PRE-EXISTING failing test in an untouched crate (crates/reports/reports_test.rs assert_eq!(1,2)) and pre-existing clippy lints in another untouched crate (crates/compute/). Branch from main touching ONLY crates/data/ (clean, green).

Run the super-dev implementation stage against a spec whose phase-1 maps to that branch's worktree with NO SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES and NO opts.testPackages. Inspect gate.ran: it records argv.join(' ') pushed before spawnSync, so you'll see 'cargo build --quiet', 'cargo test --quiet', 'cargo clippy --all-targets --quiet' — all workspace-wide, no -p flags.

Observe gate.pass === false because gate.errors contains the out-of-scope reports_test.rs failure + compute clippy lints (exit-nonzero tail). Then watch the retry loop: attempt 1 FAIL → errors fed to attempt 2 → attempt 2 FAIL (identical out-of-scope errors) → attempt 3 FAIL → log 'phase-01 failed after 3 attempts — terminating early' → allGreen=false; break (Phases 2–N skipped).

Falsify the in-scope hypothesis (proves the abort was false): run `cargo test -p data` (the only touched crate) in the same worktree → GREEN. The failures were entirely pre-existing/out-of-scope, confirming the run was false-aborted on green code.

Scoping-leak repro (confirms defect A even when env IS set): re-run step 3 with SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES=data. Inspect gate.ran: 'cargo test -p data --quiet' IS scoped (proving 97fc4df6 works for test), but 'cargo build --quiet' and 'cargo clippy --all-targets --quiet' remain workspace-wide — they still FAIL on the out-of-scope crates. This proves build+clippy must be scoped too (AC-02/AC-03), not just test.

Unit-test harness contract (from tests/build-runner-*.test.ts pattern, hermetic, no real cargo): new tests assert (a) detectTouchedCargoPackages maps 'crates/data/src/lib.rs'→['data'], dedupes preserving order, returns [] on git error / empty diff / non-crate-only diff, honors SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF; (b) scopedCargoBuildArgs([])/scopedCargoTestArgs([])/scopedCargoClippyArgs([]) are byte-identical workspace-wide argvs; (c) with scoped set {data}, a failure referencing crates/data/… is IN-SCOPE and one referencing crates/compute/… is OUT-OF-SCOPE, and inScopePass is true only when ALL failures are out-of-scope; (d) a runBuildGate integration that stubs spawnSync/git-diff so a touched 'data' crate drives build+test+clippy argvs to all carry '-p data'. npm run typecheck + npm test must stay green.

data.title: Codebase Assessment: Scope-Aware Build Gate (pi-super-dev)
data.summary: pi-super-dev is a TypeScript pi-extension (ESM, `"type":"module"`, strict tsconfig, NodeNext module resolution) — a 13-stage development pipeline orchestrator, NOT a running server. The target change is scoped to two files: `src/build-runner.ts` (the deterministic build/test/typecheck gate, a spawnSync-based side-effecting module) and `src/stages/implementation.ts` (Stage 9 per-phase TDD loop that consumes the gate). The codebase has an exceptionally well-established pattern to follow: commit 97fc4df6 already added timeout + test-scoping via a clean precedence chain (explicit opts → env var → default) implemented as PURE, fully-documented, unit-testable helpers (`resolveTimeoutMs`, `parseTestPackages`, `scopedCargoTestArgs`) plus a SHALLOW-COPY scoping step in `runBuildGate` that keeps `detectProjectCommands` byte-identical. The required work is a faithful generalization of that exact pattern: (1) add `detectTouchedCargoPackages` (one `git diff --name-only`, never throws), (2) generalize `scopedCargoTestArgs`→`scopedCargoArgs(subcommand,...)` + build/clippy wrappers, (3) scope all three rust argvs (build/test/typecheck) instead of only `:301`'s test, (4) extend `BuildGateResult` with `outOfScopeErrors`+`inScopePass` and make implementation.ts treat `inScopePass` as GREEN. Tests are vitest, test-first (RED-phase), AC/SCENARIO-tagged describe blocks, with `tmpProj`/`withEnv` helpers and assertions reading `result.ran` (argv joined pre-spawn). Verification is `npm run typecheck` + `npm test` — there is NO API/UI server to start (services omitted). Backward compatibility is paramount: every fallback (non-rust, non-git, no touched crates, unset env) must remain byte-identical to today.

patterns.name: Pure helper + precedence chain (explicit opt → env → default)
patterns.consistency: Canonical and consistent. Every tunable (timeout, test-packages) follows the same shape: a pure exported resolver, an env var documented in the module's top JSDoc, and explicit opts taking precedence including `[]` as a deliberate 'force default' signal. The new git-base-ref (`SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF`, default "main") and the auto-detected-touched-crates precedence tier MUST be added in this same style.

patterns.name: Shallow-copy scoping keeps detectProjectCommands pure/byte-identical
patterns.example: src/build-runner.ts:299-301 (`cmds0.language === "rust" && testPackages.length > 0 && cmds0.test ? { ...cmds0, test: scopedCargoTestArgs(testPackages) } : cmds0`)
patterns.consistency: Strict invariant with a dedicated regression test (build-runner-packages.test.ts 'detectProjectCommands stays pure'). Generalizing to scope build+test+typecheck MUST preserve this: build the scoped copy by spreading cmds0 and overriding the three keys, never mutate cmds0, and when the package set is empty the result must equal cmds0 exactly. Inspect detectProjectCommands's Cargo.toml branch (:206-214) for the exact base argvs: build=["cargo","build","--quiet"], test=["cargo","test","--quiet"], typecheck=["cargo","clippy","--all-targets","--quiet"].

patterns.name: spawnSync argv as discrete tokens (shell-safe, joined into ran[])
patterns.example: src/build-runner.ts:142-150 (scopedCargoTestArgs returns [...packages.flatMap(p=>["-p",p]),"--quiet"]) and :309 (`const r = spawnSync(argv[0], argv.slice(1), {cwd,timeout,encoding})`)
patterns.consistency: No `shell:true` anywhere; malicious names survive verbatim (SCENARIO-014 test). result.ran records `argv.join(" ")` BEFORE spawnSync (:308) so scoped argvs are assertable even when cargo is ENOENT — the new scoped build/clippy integration test should read result.ran exactly like the existing test-scope tests do.

patterns.name: Never-throw defensive helpers; degrade to safe default
patterns.consistency: Consistent across the module. detectTouchedCargoPackages MUST follow: wrap the git diff in try/catch, and on ANY git error / empty stdout / non-crate paths return [] (→ workspace-wide fallback). inScopePass classifier must likewise never throw.

patterns.name: Test-first vitest, AC/SCENARIO-tagged, env save/restore, no real toolchain
patterns.example: tests/build-runner-packages.test.ts:24-32 (tmpProj + withEnv helpers) and :193-280 (runBuildGate precedence tests read result.ran)
patterns.consistency: Established and uniform. New tests for touched-crate detection, scoped build/test/clippy argv construction, in-scope classification, and the git-diff-mocked runBuildGate integration MUST match this style: tmpProj() to make a throwaway dir, writeFileSync for manifests, withEnv() to save/restore SUPER_DEV_* env vars, describe names tagged 'AC-NN', and assert via result.ran/errors rather than spawning real cargo/git where possible. For the git-diff mock, prefer an in-tree fake 'crates/<pkg>/' layout in a tmp git repo OR spy on spawnSync per the existing pi-spawn.test.ts mocking approach.

patterns.name: Stage retry loop: bounded attempts, feed prior errors forward, terminate-early on genuine failure
patterns.example: src/stages/implementation.ts:43-78 (for attempt 1..3 → tdd → impl → runBuildGate → break on gate.pass; `attemptErrors = gate.errors` threaded into next attempt's prompt at :55-58; :74-78 break on `!green`)
patterns.consistency: Single canonical consumer. The change is minimal: line :64 result must be read for both `.pass` and `.inScopePass`; line :66-73 log block gains an IN-SCOPE GREEN branch; line :74 `if(!green)` becomes the ONLY termination, where green = gate.pass || gate.inScopePass. Keep ControlObj shape stable (phasesCompleted/totalPhases/allGreen/filesModified/summary) — prior lessons flag malformed control as a top failure cause.

recommendations: Mirror commit 97fc4df6's structure exactly: export detectTouchedCargoPackages, scopedCargoArgs(subcommand,packages,extraArgs?), scopedCargoBuildArgs, scopedCargoClippyArgs as pure JSDoc'd helpers; keep scopedCargoTestArgs as a 1-line wrapper so its existing tests (build-runner-packages.test.ts:111-160) still pass unchanged. Put the SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF doc block next to the SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES block in the module top-comment so the precedence story (explicit → env → auto-touched → workspace-wide) reads as one narrative.

For the three-command scoping, build ONE scoped cmds object in runBuildGate: `if (cmds0.language==='rust' && packages.length>0) cmds = { ...cmds0, build: scopedCargoBuildArgs(packages), test: scopedCargoTestArgs(packages), typecheck: scopedCargoClippyArgs(packages) } else cmds = cmds0`. This keeps the empty-packages path byte-identical (cmds0) and satisfies the 'non-crate paths / non-git → [] → workspace-wide' acceptance criterion with zero extra branching.

Implement the in-scope classifier as a pure helper classifyGateErrors(errors, scopedPackages): { inScopeErrors, outOfScopeErrors } keyed off two regexes: `crates/<pkg>/` from `--> path`/failure-line paths AND `-p <pkg>` markers — and only classify a line OUT-OF-SCOPE when EVERY matched crate is absent from scopedPackages. Compute inScopePass = !pass && errors.length>0 && inScopeErrors.length===0. Add it to BuildGateResult as additive fields so non-rust/non-failure paths are unaffected (inScopePass irrelevant when pass:true).

In implementation.ts make the smallest possible edit: `const green = gate.pass || gate.inScopePass;` computed right after the runBuildGate call (:64-66), add an explicit IN-SCOPE GREEN log line naming the ignored crates, and leave the `if(!green){...break;}` structure intact. Do NOT touch ControlObj, the budget check, the phase loop, or filesModified aggregation — the malformed-control lessons (filesModified char-split) make minimalism here a hard safety constraint. Add future-work note on full main-baseline-diff (#3) only in the implementation summary doc, not code.

data.title: Scope-Aware Build Gate — Auto-scope cargo gate to touched crates and classify in-scope vs pre-existing out-of-scope failures
data.summary:
TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION.

Problem: The super-dev build-gate (`src/build-runner.ts#runBuildGate`) runs `cargo build && cargo test && cargo clippy --all-targets` WORKSPACE-WIDE. On messy real-world Rust monorepos (evidence: failed run 2026-07-19 on stockfan-server spec 54), pre-existing failures in crates the current branch never touched (e.g. `reports_test.rs`, `stockfan-compute` clippy lints, `job_queries_test.rs`) flip the gate FAIL. The implementation retry loop (`src/stages/implementation.ts`) treats every gate FAIL identically, and after `MAX_ATTEMPTS=3` binary-terminates ALL remaining phases ("terminating early"). Net: Stage 9 aborts on green in-scope code, so Phases 2–6 never run. Two root causes: (a) commit 97fc4df6 only scopes the `test` argv (`build-runner.ts:301` overrides only `cmds.test`), leaving `build` and `clippy`/typecheck workspace-wide; (b) the retry loop has no concept of in-scope vs out-of-scope failures.

Scope: Pure TypeScript change to `src/build-runner.ts` + `src/stages/implementation.ts` (+ co-located tests). No changes to `nodes.ts`, `workflow.ts`, `pipeline.ts`, render templates, or the control-flow engine. No new runtime deps; no new spawned processes beyond existing gate commands plus one `git diff --merge-only`.

Solution (4 capabilities):
1. Auto-detect touched cargo crates via `git -C <cwd> diff --merge-base <baseRef> --name-only` (AC-01) and map `crates/<pkg>/…` → `<pkg>`, deduped, never throwing.
2. Scope ALL THREE gate commands (build + test + clippy) — not just test — by generalizing `scopedCargoTestArgs` into a shared `scopedCargoArgs(subcommand, packages, extraArgs?)` family (AC-02) and overriding `build`/`test`/`typecheck` on the shallow `cmds` copy (AC-03).
3. Classify gate failures as in-scope vs out-of-scope using `crates/<pkg>/` and `-p <pkg>` markers, adding `outOfScopeErrors: string[]` + `inScopePass: boolean` to `BuildGateResult` (AC-04).
4. Treat `gate.pass || gate.inScopePass` as phase-GREEN in the retry loop, logging ignored pre-existing failures, and terminate-early ONLY on genuine in-scope failures (AC-05).

Constraints/precedence: Package-set resolution precedence (highest→lowest): explicit `opts.testPackages`/`opts.packages` (incl. explicit `[]` = force workspace-wide) → `process.env.SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES` → `detectTouchedCargoPackages(cwd)` → workspace-wide. `SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF` overrides the base ref (default `"main"`). Git-diff is skipped when a higher-precedence source is present. The classifier is conservative: on any ambiguity an error is treated IN-SCOPE (never grants a false green). Backward-compatibility: non-Cargo repos, non-git dirs, no-touched-crates, and unset env vars produce IDENTICAL argvs and an IDENTICAL `runBuildGate` result to today (modulo two additive fields `outOfScopeErrors`/`inScopePass`, which never block when there are zero failures or no scoping is active).

All 7 ACs (AC-01…AC-07) addressed; all 29 scenarios (SCENARIO-001…SCENARIO-029) mapped. Acceptance: `npm run typecheck` (tsc --noEmit, strict) clean and `npm test` (vitest run) green for existing + new tests.

Out of scope (future work, to be noted in 10-implementation-summary.md): Full baseline-diff (#3 — running the gate on `main` and subtracting the failure set). The in-scope classification (#4) covers the common pre-existing-failure case more cheaply; baseline-diff remains a future enhancement for edge cases (e.g. failures with no parseable crate marker).
data.architecture:
ARCHITECTURE. Two files change; the module boundary (build-runner as the side-effecting deterministic oracle; implementation.ts as the per-phase retry driver) is preserved.

### Module: src/build-runner.ts

**(1) Touched-crate detection — AC-01.** Add `export function detectTouchedCargoPackages(cwd: string, baseRef?: string): string[]`. Contract: input `cwd` (absolute worktree path) and optional `baseRef` (defaults to `process.env.SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF` if set else `"main"`). Behaviour: wrap a `spawnSync("git", ["-C", cwd, "diff", "--merge-base", baseRef, "--name-only"], { encoding: "utf8" })` in `try { } catch { return []; }`. On `r.status !== 0`, `r.error`, empty stdout, or any thrown error → `return []`. Otherwise parse stdout lines; for each line, match the FIRST `crates/<pkg>/` segment via regex `/(?:^|\/)crates\/([^/]+)\//` (or split on `/` and detect `crates` segment followed by the package name); map each matching line to `<pkg>`; lines with no `crates/` segment are ignored (non-crate paths, e.g. root `Cargo.toml`, `README`). Dedupe with the existing `dedupePreservingOrder` helper preserving first-seen order. MUST NEVER throw (the entire body is try/caught). Pure wrt argv construction but reads env + spawns git (side-effecting) — same category as the rest of the module.

**(2) Scoped argv family — AC-02.** Add shared `export function scopedCargoArgs(subcommand: "build" | "test" | "clippy", packages: string[], extraArgs?: string[]): string[]` emitting `["cargo", subcommand, ...packages.flatMap(p => ["-p", p]), ...(extraArgs ?? [])]`. Refactor `scopedCargoTestArgs(packages)` into a thin wrapper: `return scopedCargoArgs("test", packages, ["--quiet"])` — IDENTICAL output to today (non-empty → `["cargo","test","-p",p…,"--quiet"]`; empty → `["cargo","test","--quiet"]`), so `verify.ts`/`implementation.ts` callers and existing tests are unchanged. Add `export function scopedCargoBuildArgs(packages: string[]): string[]` → `scopedCargoArgs("build", packages, ["--quiet"])` and `export function scopedCargoClippyArgs(packages: string[]): string[]` → `scopedCargoArgs("clippy", packages, ["--all-targets", "--quiet"])`. Empty-set invariants: all three helpers return byte-identical workspace-wide argv (`["cargo","build","--quiet"]`, `["cargo","test","--quiet"]`, `["cargo","clippy","--all-targets","--quiet"]`) so the no-scoping path is unchanged.

**(3) runBuildGate auto-scoping of all three commands — AC-03.** Replace the current `testPackages` resolution block (lines ~286-301) with a FOUR-tier precedence resolver: (i) `opts.testPackages !== undefined` → `dedupePreservingOrder(opts.testPackages)` (explicit `[]` forces workspace-wide, and the git-diff spawn is SKIPPED); (ii) else if `process.env.SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES` set → `parseTestPackages(...)`; (iii) else if `cmds0.language === "rust"` → `detectTouchedCargoPackages(cwd)`; (iv) else → `[]` (workspace-wide). Note: git-diff MUST NOT run when tier (i) or (ii) supplies a value (no wasted spawn). Then, when `cmds0.language === "rust" && resolvedSet.length > 0`, build a shallow-copy override that replaces ALL THREE of `cmds.build`, `cmds.test`, `cmds.typecheck` (currently only `cmds.test` is replaced at :301): `cmds = { ...cmds0, build: scopedCargoBuildArgs(set), test: scopedCargoTestArgs(set), typecheck: scopedCargoClippyArgs(set) }`. When `set.length === 0` → `cmds = cmds0` byte-identical to today (no override). `detectProjectCommands` stays PURE — scoping is applied only on the shallow copy, preserving the detector regression assertion. The three callers (`verify.ts:87`, `implementation.ts:64`, `stages/index.ts:53`) still pass only `{ signal }` — scoping is derived internally, call signatures unchanged.

**(4) In-scope classification — AC-04.** Extend `BuildGateResult` with two additive fields: `outOfScopeErrors: string[]` and `inScopePass: boolean`. After the `exec` loop collects `errors`, run a pure `function classifyOutOfScopeErrors(errors: string[], scopedSet: string[]): { inScopeErrors: string[]; outOfScopeErrors: string[] }`. Contract: for each error block, extract referenced crate names via (a) `--> <path>` / `--> crates/<pkg>/…` markers using regex `crates\/([^/]+)\//`, and (b) cargo test-failure `-p <pkg>` markers using regex `/(?:^|\s)-p\s+(\S+)/`. An error is OUT-OF-SCOPE iff it references ≥1 crate AND EVERY referenced crate is NOT in `scopedSet`; otherwise (no marker found, or ≥1 referenced crate IS in scope, or `scopedSet` is empty) → IN-SCOPE (conservative; ambiguity never grants false green). Compute `inScopePass = !pass && errors.length > 0 && outOfScopeErrors.length === errors.length` (ALL failures out-of-scope/pre-existing). When `pass === true`, set `inScopePass = true` and `outOfScopeErrors = []` (classification is a no-op — the gate already passed). The classifier MUST NEVER throw (try/catch the whole pass; on any error treat all as in-scope). When no scoping is active (`scopedSet` empty / non-rust), `outOfScopeErrors = []` and `inScopePass` stays false for any failure → current abort semantics preserved exactly.

### Module: src/stages/implementation.ts

**(5) In-scope verdict in retry loop — AC-05.** After `const gate = runBuildGate(setup.worktreePath, { signal: ctx.signal })` (line 64, signature unchanged), compute the phase's GREEN condition as `gate.pass || gate.inScopePass`. When green via `inScopePass`, emit a distinct log line: ``ctx.log(`Implementation ${phaseId} IN-SCOPE GREEN on attempt ${attempt} — ${gate.outOfScopeErrors.length} pre-existing out-of-scope failure(s) ignored (crates: ${cratesFromErrors(gate.outOfScopeErrors).join(",")})`)``. Keep the existing PASS/FAIL log. The `if (!green) { … "failed after N attempts — terminating early"; allGreen=false; break; }` path fires ONLY when NEITHER `pass` nor `inScopePass` holds after `MAX_ATTEMPTS` (genuine in-scope failures). This stops the false-abort while still terminating on real in-scope breakage. (`cratesFromErrors` is a small local helper reusing the `crates/<pkg>/` + `-p <pkg>` extraction, or the out-of-scope crate list is precomputed and stored on the result to avoid re-parsing — implementation detail, prefer storing the resolved scoped crate set on the result for the log.)

### Data flow / invariant preservation
- `detectProjectCommands` purity: unchanged; all overrides applied on shallow copies only.
- Workspace-wide byte-identity: empty scoped set ⇒ argvs and result identical to pre-change (modulo two additive fields that never block).
- Process budget: +1 spawn only in the auto-touched tier (iii) for rust repos with no explicit/env override; all other tiers add zero spawns. No `shell:true` ever; package names are discrete argv elements.
- The three existing callers are call-site-compatible (still `{ signal }`); no signature change propagates.
data.testingStrategy:
TESTING STRATEGY. Primary oracle: deterministic unit + integration tests in `src/build-runner.test.ts` (new file) and a focused `implementation` stage test, executed by `npm test` (vitest run) + `npm run typecheck` (tsc --noEmit strict). No new runtime deps; `spawnSync` and `git` are stubbed/mocked so tests are hermetic and fast.

(a) `detectTouchedCargoPackages` (AC-01 → SCENARIO-001/002/003/020/022/023): stub `spawnSync` to return synthetic `git diff --name-only` stdout; assert `crates/data/src/lib.rs` + `crates/api/src/main.rs` → `["data","api"]` (order preserved, deduped); `crates/data/a.rs` + `crates/data/b.rs` → `["data"]` (dedupe); duplicate `crates/data/…` lines → single entry; env `SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF=develop` flows into the `--merge-base develop` argv; non-crate-only diff (`Cargo.toml`, `README.md`) → `[]`; empty diff / `r.status!==0` / thrown error → `[]`; assert the helper NEVER throws (wrap a throwing stub, expect `[]`). Prefer injecting the spawn function or spying on `child_process.spawnSync` so the assertion reads the actual argv.

(b) Scoped argv family (AC-02 → SCENARIO-004/005): assert `scopedCargoBuildArgs(["data","api"])` == `["cargo","build","-p","data","-p","api","--quiet"]`; `scopedCargoTestArgs(["data"])` == `["cargo","test","-p","data","--quiet"]` (unchanged); `scopedCargoClippyArgs(["data"])` == `["cargo","clippy","-p","data","--all-targets","--quiet"]`; empty-set → byte-identical workspace-wide forms for all three; assert `scopedCargoTestArgs` still returns identical output to the pre-refactor implementation (regression of existing call sites).

(c) runBuildGate auto-scoping (AC-03 → SCENARIO-006/007/008/017): integration test that stubs `spawnSync` (a) to return a touched-`data` git-diff on the first call family and (b) to capture/succeed the build/test/clippy spawns; in a temp `Cargo.toml` worktree with no env and no explicit packages, assert the captured build, test, AND typecheck argvs all carry `-p data`. Precedence tests: explicit `opts.testPackages=["api"]` ⇒ git-diff spawn NEVER happens and `-p api` used; `SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES="api"` set ⇒ `-p api` used; explicit `[]` ⇒ workspace-wide argvs and no git spawn.

(d) In-scope classification (AC-04 → SCENARIO-009/010/011/021/024/028): unit-test the pure classifier with scoped set `{data}`: an error block referencing `crates/data/src/lib.rs` is IN-SCOPE; an error block referencing only `crates/compute/src/lib.rs` is OUT-OF-SCOPE; an error with NO parseable crate marker is IN-SCOPE (conservative); an error referencing both `data` and `compute` is IN-SCOPE (mixed). `inScopePass` true iff ALL errors out-of-scope and `!pass`; `inScopePass` true and `outOfScopeErrors=[]` when `pass`; empty scoped set ⇒ `outOfScopeErrors=[]`, `inScopePass=false` for any failure. Use realistic cargo error strings (`error[E0308]: … --> crates/compute/src/jobs.rs:42:10`, `failures: -p compute --test job_queries_test`).

(e) implementation.ts retry loop (AC-05 → SCENARIO-012/013/014/025/027): test the stage with a stubbed `runBuildGate` returning `{pass:false, inScopePass:true, outOfScopeErrors:[…compute…]}` on attempt 1 → assert phase is GREEN, the IN-SCOPE GREEN log line is emitted, no early termination, and the commit proceeds; a result with `{pass:false, inScopePass:false}` for 3 attempts → assert "terminating early" + `allGreen=false` + loop breaks; a `{pass:true}` result → normal GREEN path.

(f) Backward-compat / non-regression (AC-06 → SCENARIO-015/016/026): non-cargo repo (node/go/python manifests) ⇒ argvs identical, result identical modulo two additive fields; non-git / no-touched-crates / unset env ⇒ workspace-wide argvs; zero failures ⇒ `inScopePass` never blocks.

Gate: `npm run typecheck` must be clean (strict); `npm test` must pass for existing AND new suites. No new runtime dependencies; no network; no real `git`/`cargo` execution in CI (all spawned).
